findings.blocker: After iter-078's `intro r y; funext j`, every route to evaluate the RHS smul componentwise stalls because the smul on `↑scK₀.X_2` is mediated by `h_mod_pi₂.toSMul` (built via `Pi.module` with anonymous per-i builders), not literal `Pi.instSMul`. `simp/rw [Pi.smul_apply]` fail with 'did not find the pattern'; `letI` introducing a parallel `∀ i, Module R (Z_2 i)` does not bridge to the existing `h_mod_pi₂`-mediated smul. Only `change … = h_mod_pi₂.toSMul.smul r _ j` succeeds (defeq), but the next step (evaluating that smul componentwise) hits the same anonymous-builder wall.

The differential `(scK₀.f).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))` is not literally `r • (scK₀.f).hom (e₁.symm y)` — closing requires the S2 5-layer dsimp + S3 alternating-sum exposure + per-summand RingHom distributivity (the iter-073 recipe). The `change` only normalises the smul name; the substantive proof remains.

next_steps: Iter-080 should refactor `h_mod_pi₁/₂/₃` (BasicOpenCech.lean L911/921/931) to use a `letI perI_n : ∀ i, Module R (Z_n i) := …` *first*, then `have h_mod_pi_n : Module R (∀ i, Z_n i) := Pi.module _ _ _` (or `inferInstance`). After this refactor, `Pi.smul_apply` fires directly. This is not a 'new project-local helper lemma' — it's a literal expansion of the existing `Pi.module` construction. Then complete S2 (5-layer `dsimp`) → S3 (`objD` identification) → S4 (`Pi.smul_apply` on LHS) → S5 (`Finset.smul_sum`) → S6 (`Finset.sum_congr`) → S7 (per-summand RingHom distributivity) → S8 (`rfl`).

The simp lemma is stale.

findings.blocker: (only the single permitted gap-fill `instIsMonoidal_W` remains) — substantive iso-stability of sheafification under presheaf-tensor whiskering. Routes blocked: (a) decomposition via `tensorHom_def` reduces to `IsMonoidal.whiskerLeft/whiskerRight` (no progress, same condition); (b) reduction via `sheafificationCompToSheaf` requires `toPresheaf R₀` to preserve tensors, which it does NOT (presheaf-of-modules tensor is `R₀(U)`-balanced while the abelian-presheaf tensor is ℤ-tensor); (c) closedness route (`Sites/Monoidal.lean` template) requires `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` + `HasFunctorEnrichedHom` for the varying-ring case, which is absent from Mathlib (only the fixed-ring `ModuleCat R` case exists, in `Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Closed.lean`).

attempts.insight: Reduction is mechanical; the substantive content (iso-stability under whiskering) is the single permitted gap-fill. `tensorHom_def` route reduces to `whiskerLeft`/`whiskerRight` stability — same condition. `sheafificationCompToSheaf` route is blocked because `toPresheaf R₀` is NOT monoidal (R₀(U)-balanced vs ℤ-tensor mismatch). Closedness-via-internal-hom route requires `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` for varying-ring R₀, which is absent from Mathlib.

next_steps: Iter-080+ for `instIsMonoidal_W` closure: cleanest long-term is to upstream `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` to Mathlib + `HasFunctorEnrichedHom` for the varying-ring case, then `Sites/Monoidal.lean`'s approach transfers directly (multi-PR effort). Stopgap: a stalks-level argument (`Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective`/`Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective` on `1 ⊗ f`) may be tractable in ~100–200 LOC. The `instIsMonoidal_W` sorry does NOT block downstream uses of `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` — only consumers of `(W X).IsMonoidal` as a hypothesis (rare). Phase C step C1 (`LineBundle` refactor to invertible 𝒪_X-modules) can now proceed.
